steps.title: Find the side length
steps.content:
An equilateral triangle has $3$ equal sides, so divide the perimeter by $3$.
$$\frac{894}{3}=298$$

steps.title: Use the circumradius formula
steps.content:
For an equilateral triangle, the radius of the circumscribed circle is
$$R=\frac{s\sqrt{3}}{3}$$
Substitute $s=298$:
$$R=\frac{298\sqrt{3}}{3}$$

steps.title: Read the coefficient
steps.content: The radius is written as $w\sqrt{3}$, so $w$ is the coefficient of $\sqrt{3}$. The answer is 298/3.
